topics: Research and explain three real-world IoT applications using the components available in the IoT Trainer kit. Set up the IoT Trainer kit and understand its modules.
Use the temperature and humidity sensor module in the IoT Trainer kit to collect environmental data. Write a script to display the readings on the provided interface.
Interface the light and sound sensors from the IoT Trainer kit. Write a script to capture data and trigger actions, such as lighting an LED when the sound level exceeds a threshold.
Use the IoT Trainer kit to set up an MQTT communication system. Publish sensor data to a topic and subscribe to another topic to receive control signals.
Use the IoT Trainer kit to send sensor data to a local server via HTTP. Create a simple web interface to display the data.
Write a script to control an LED and relay module in the IoT Trainer kit. Implement a scenario where the relay controls a fan based on temperature readings.
Utilize the PWM functionality in the IoT Trainer kit to control the speed of a motor. Implement a system where motor speed adjusts based on sensor input, such as light intensity.
Configure the IoT Trainer kit to send sensor data to a local server for storage and visualization. Use basic analytics to process the data.
Write a script to analyze sensor data from the IoT Trainer kit, such as calculating averages or identifying trends. Visualize the results.
Implement basic encryption techniques using the IoT Trainer kit to secure data transmission. Demonstrate how data can be encrypted and decrypted.
Project: Design and implement a project that uses multiple components of the IoT Trainer kit to create a functional IoT system, such as a smart home system.
